jokes.text: Hillary Clinton is president, sir! I thought I would just try to scare you out of it.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to wake up his unconscious boss with shocking news
jokes.punchline: Admitting it was a lie to scare him awake
jokes.explanation: Sharp setup/punchline with perfect character voice - Jack would absolutely think Hillary Clinton as president is horrifying enough to wake someone from a coma

jokes.text: This morning, I had to cut my own cantaloupe.
jokes.setup: Jack listing his workplace humiliations
jokes.punchline: Treating self-service fruit preparation as ultimate degradation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Jack's privileged perspective makes cantaloupe-cutting seem like manual labor. Brilliant economy and specificity.

jokes.text: 12! You've got to wake up, please!
jokes.setup: Jack being moved from floor 52 to a lower floor
jokes.punchline: Treating floor 12 like being banished to hell
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's corporate hierarchy obsession, though fairly straightforward

jokes.text: Oh, God. Marcus Schenkenberg was there. Who?
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning an obscure reference from her dream
jokes.punchline: Pete's confused 'Who?' highlighting the randomness
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from obscure 90s model reference that most wouldn't know

jokes.text: You were using your treadmill? Yes, and, yes, Pete, I use it every day.
jokes.setup: Pete's surprised question about treadmill use
jokes.punchline: Liz's defensive response revealing she doesn't actually use it much
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment about Liz's fitness lies, though not particularly sharp

jokes.text: JetFun. Is that the one with the footbaths? Oh, no, that's AirBike. They also have in-flight pornos and NBC news. JetFun's the one that hands out fresh popcorn.
jokes.setup: Discussion of fake airline amenities
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd airline features including NBC news as an amenity
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist worldbuilding with meta-joke about NBC, though slightly rambling

jokes.text: some Rust Belt tramp answered the phone
jokes.setup: Liz describing calling Floyd in Cleveland
jokes.punchline: Classist dismissal of Cleveland woman as 'Rust Belt tramp'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon line - combining geographic snobbery with casual cruelty in economic phrasing

jokes.text: I want to be top dog for once instead of just... dog.
jokes.setup: Liz wanting to be in control with Floyd
jokes.punchline: Reducing herself from 'top dog' to just 'dog'
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating wordplay that captures Liz's low self-esteem perfectly

jokes.text: For once, I am not gonna be Jan Brady. I'm gonna be Marcia, damn it. Oh, my nose! My bad!
jokes.setup: Liz declaring she'll be the favored sister like Marcia Brady
jokes.punchline: Immediately recreating Marcia's football-to-nose incident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pop culture reference with perfect physical comedy timing - declaring she'll be Marcia then immediately getting hurt like Marcia

jokes.text: Once a year, the Teamsters go to this Italian sandwich shop in Brooklyn. No one knows what it's called or where it is. It's a Teamster secret.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining Sandwich Day tradition
jokes.punchline: Treating sandwich procurement like a mystical secret society ritual
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist premise treating mundane sandwich delivery like ancient mystery, perfect for Kenneth's character

jokes.text: And the dipping sauce. Oh, joy! God bless us, every one!
jokes.setup: Kenneth's religious reverence for the sandwiches
jokes.punchline: Quoting Tiny Tim from A Christmas Carol about sandwich sauce
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - combining his southern religiosity with Dickensian reference over sandwich sauce

jokes.text: By the way, you know who hates unicorns? Mark Wahlberg.
jokes.setup: Jack ending a business call
jokes.punchline: Random fact about Mark Wahlberg and unicorns
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurdist non-sequitur - completely random but specific enough to be memorable

jokes.text: I've been sleeping with the Cindy Crawford of corporations for the last 22 years. What am I supposed to do? Just lie down with some skank like 3M?
jokes.setup: Jack describing his loyalty to GE
jokes.punchline: Comparing corporations to women using crude sexual metaphors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack line - perfectly captures his corporate obsession and casual misogyny with great metaphor work

jokes.text: Good God, I'd rather work for an American car company than jump on that sinking ship.
jokes.setup: Suggestion that Jack work for Bush administration
jokes.punchline: Calling Bush administration worse than failing American auto industry
jokes.explanation: Solid political joke for 2008, comparing two failing institutions with Jack's characteristic snobbishness

jokes.text: I will remember you? Will you remember me? Doobie-doo, doobie-doo? Don't let your life pass you by?
jokes.setup: Jonathan trying to comfort Jack with a song
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly singing Sarah McLachlan while Jack suffers
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Jonathan's inappropriate musical comfort attempt

jokes.text: I got a company-wide e-mail from the new CEO that was just a link to cats wearing bow ties.
jokes.setup: Liz reporting on new CEO's communications
jokes.punchline: Professional email being just cat photos
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist corporate humor - specific image of cats in bow ties makes it memorable

jokes.text: 12? Did I hit 12? I wanted, uh... 4012, which is 52, where I work. Business.
jokes.setup: Jack caught going to his demoted floor
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous lie about wanting floor 4012 (which doesn't exist)
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy - Jack's panic leading to obviously impossible lie, ending with defeated 'Business.'

jokes.text: Since my surgery, I have to eat slowly or my insides might explode.
jokes.setup: Lutz explaining why he's eating slowly
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of potential internal explosion
jokes.explanation: Good Lutz character moment - matter-of-fact delivery of grotesque medical detail

jokes.text: Give me it! It's like knives!
jokes.setup: Tracy grabbing Lutz's sandwich
jokes.punchline: Lutz's pain described as 'like knives'
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy escalation, though fairly straightforward

jokes.text: Where's my mac and cheese?! But as far as Liz Lemon knows, her sandwich never came.
jokes.setup: Frank referencing Liz's violent food reactions
jokes.punchline: Immediately cutting to plan to hide sandwich theft
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure showing the writers' fear of Liz's food rage

jokes.text: I'm pretty tired from playing as hard as I work.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to seem cool to Floyd
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at seeming like a party person
jokes.explanation: Good Liz character moment - trying to sound sophisticated but landing on corporate cliché

jokes.text: You know, maybe we hit that barbecue place you puked at. You'll have to be more specific.
jokes.setup: Floyd suggesting a specific restaurant
jokes.punchline: Liz has puked at multiple barbecue places
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline revealing Liz's romantic history through her vomiting history - economical and character-specific

jokes.text: Oh, somebody get me out of this dress! I can't breathe!
jokes.setup: Liz trying to look good for Floyd
jokes.punchline: Immediately suffering in uncomfortable clothing
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy about uncomfortable formal wear, decent character moment

jokes.text: Where's my sandwich?! Lutz made us do it! No, it was Frank. No, it was you! I'm a patsy?
jokes.setup: Liz discovering her missing sandwich
jokes.punchline: Writers immediately turning on each other, Lutz's confused 'I'm a patsy?'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with classic blame-shifting, Lutz's innocent confusion adds to the chaos

jokes.text: the only thing necessary for the triumph of evil is for good men to do nothing. Please ask my permission before you quote me, Kenneth.
jokes.setup: Kenneth quoting famous Edmund Burke line about evil
jokes.punchline: Liz thinking Kenneth is quoting himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant joke about Liz's narcissism and Kenneth's earnestness - she thinks he's claiming credit for famous quote

jokes.text: I don't know how, but you're gonna get me another sandwich, or I'm gonna cut your face up so bad, you'll have a chin. You'll all have chins!
jokes.setup: Liz threatening the writers over her sandwich
jokes.punchline: Bizarre threat that having a chin is somehow bad, extended to everyone
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurdist threat - completely nonsensical anatomy-based insult that becomes more confusing when extended

jokes.text: Failure.
jokes.setup: Jack riding elevator to his demoted floor
jokes.punchline: Elevator announcing 'Failure' instead of floor number
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag with maximum economy - single word that perfectly captures Jack's state

jokes.text: There was actually a tornado in Cleveland last week. Destroyed an entire city block... three bowling alleys, a liquor store, and the liquor-store museum.
jokes.setup: Floyd describing tornado damage in Cleveland
jokes.punchline: Revealing Cleveland has a liquor-store museum
jokes.explanation: Good escalating joke about Cleveland stereotypes - the liquor-store museum is the perfect absurd detail

jokes.text: I haven't stayed up this late since college. And behind this trapdoor, more Orcs.
jokes.setup: Liz claiming it's late at 4:30
jokes.punchline: Random Dungeons & Dragons reference revealing her college activities
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation - Liz's 'wild' college days were D&D campaigns, explains her early bedtime

jokes.text: I read that you guys are getting an lkea. Yeah, I'll believe it when I see it. We've just been burned before.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning Cleveland getting an IKEA
jokes.punchline: Floyd's weary response about being 'burned before' by retail promises
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor about second-tier cities and retail disappointment - Floyd's weary resignation is perfect

jokes.text: Now turn and look back at me. Attaboy. Eat it up, Cleveland.
jokes.setup: Liz watching Floyd leave
jokes.punchline: Directing him like a movie scene, then gloating to an entire city
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - narcissistic fantasy direction followed by petty regional superiority

jokes.text: I even stopped to catch a snowflake with my tongue, but, evidently, that's some sort of signal in Chelsea.
jokes.setup: Jack describing his confused wandering in snow
jokes.punchline: Innocent action being misinterpreted as sexual signal in gay neighborhood
jokes.explanation: Great Jack line combining his naivety about gay culture with perfect comedic misdirection

jokes.text: I was hit by lightning as a child. They made a movie about me.
jokes.setup: Jack asking about the doctor's voice
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of being a lightning-strike movie subject
jokes.explanation: Perfect 30 Rock random character backstory - matter-of-fact delivery of completely absurd biography

jokes.text: a tornado hit downtown Detroit, putting out several fires.
jokes.setup: News report about tornado damage
jokes.punchline: Tornado actually helping Detroit by putting out fires
jokes.explanation: Dark joke about Detroit's urban decay - natural disaster as improvement rather than destruction

jokes.text: The poor guy got Lemoned... hard.
jokes.setup: Liz describing her effect on Floyd
jokes.punchline: Using her own name as a verb for romantic failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific wordplay - Liz turning her romantic disasters into a verb with perfect self-awareness

jokes.text: No, Jenna, I did not come back from the bathroom and hand him my underwear. That's how I met that mobster.
jokes.setup: Jenna suggesting seduction technique
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation about Jenna's mobster relationship origin
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character moment - casual mention of criminal romantic history as relationship advice

jokes.text: You with the sport coat, you're the girl.
jokes.setup: Teamster organizing fake romantic scene
jokes.punchline: Arbitrarily assigning Kenneth as the female role
jokes.explanation: Decent blue-collar humor about gender roles, straightforward but functional

jokes.text: After my junior year at Oberlin, we road-tripped to South by Southwest.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to relate to truckers
jokes.punchline: Comparing privileged college road trip to long-haul trucking
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Frank's oblivious privilege - equating music festival trip with trucking life

jokes.text: We're not an airline anymore. Wait, wait, wait. One storm? That's all it takes? We're still gonna make popcorn. Just no more airplanes.
jokes.setup: Floyd's flight being cancelled
jokes.punchline: Entire airline shutting down, but still committed to popcorn service
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - airline going out of business but maintaining brand identity through popcorn

jokes.text: A Chinese character that she thinks means 'peace,' but it really means 'I have chlamydia'?
jokes.setup: Liz describing generic tattooed girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Specific mistranslation joke about STD announcement
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about bad tattoo tropes with specific medical twist

jokes.text: This is more important than me getting home or my partner meeting with Peter Venkman.
jokes.setup: Floyd prioritizing conversation with Liz
jokes.punchline: Casual Ghostbusters reference as real business meeting
jokes.explanation: Mild pop culture reference humor - treating fictional character as real business contact

jokes.text: And I can't have booze because I'm on antibiotics after having some skanky club sex. He's on antibiotics because he lost his lucky penny and assumed it fell in his ear again.
jokes.setup: Frank's seemingly reasonable medical excuse
jokes.punchline: Revealing Lutz's bizarre penny-in-ear assumption
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Frank's crude overshare followed by Lutz's completely bizarre medical logic

jokes.text: Your surgery was for an undescended testicle. Wrong again. It was for two undescended testicles.
jokes.setup: Frank correcting Lutz about his surgery
jokes.punchline: Lutz proudly correcting that it was both testicles
jokes.explanation: Good Lutz character moment - taking pride in having worse medical problems than expected

jokes.text: Peter Venkman... That's from 'Ghostbusters'! You used 'Ghostbusters' for evil!
jokes.setup: Liz realizing Floyd's lie about business meeting
jokes.punchline: Treating Ghostbusters reference as moral corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz reaction - treating pop culture lies as genuine moral failing with appropriate outrage

jokes.text: I'm the Michael Clayton of Cleveland.
jokes.setup: Floyd defending his status
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to George Clooney character in regional context
jokes.explanation: Good character line about regional status anxiety - perfect movie reference for Floyd's pretensions

jokes.text: Well, I hope your car blows up. I'll move to Cleveland when you get that lkea. Never!
jokes.setup: Liz and Floyd arguing
jokes.punchline: Escalating from car explosion to IKEA denial
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - turning retail promises into relationship weapon

jokes.text: You are vindictive, Liz Lemon!
jokes.setup: Floyd's angry departure
jokes.punchline: Using her full name like an angry parent
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation moment - formal name usage adding gravity to silly argument

jokes.text: This smells just like Hill People Milk. I've been drinking this since I was a baby!
jokes.setup: Kenneth smelling the alcohol
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation about drinking mysterious hill people beverage as infant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth backstory moment - casually revealing bizarre Appalachian upbringing detail

jokes.text: You never know when someone's going to slip into a coma and leave you regretting all the things you didn't say or get in writing from the company's lawyers.
jokes.setup: Jack giving life advice about regret
jokes.punchline: Turning heartfelt advice into corporate legal concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack misdirection - starting with genuine emotion, ending with corporate paranoia

jokes.text: What I'm trying to say is you're... young and you still haven't blown it completely, so don't... start now.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to give Liz relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Frames 'not completely ruined' as encouragement with hesitant delivery
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's awkward attempt at supportiveness. The hesitation sells it.

jokes.text: That's sort of a cliché. You're sort of a cliché.
jokes.setup: TSA agent commenting on airport chase
jokes.punchline: Liz's weak comeback
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-humor about romantic comedy tropes with weak Liz response

jokes.text: You're choosing a sandwich over a guy. Hmm. That is less clichéd.
jokes.setup: Liz hesitating between sandwich and pursuit
jokes.punchline: TSA agent appreciating the unexpected choice
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on subverting romantic comedy expectations through food obsession

jokes.text: I can have it all!
jokes.setup: Liz deciding to eat sandwich while running
jokes.punchline: Feminist declaration while literally choking on food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - feminist empowerment undercut by physical comedy disaster

jokes.text: God, lady, you're eating foil!
jokes.setup: Liz frantically eating sandwich while running
jokes.punchline: Revealing she's literally consuming packaging
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation - Liz's desperation leading to literal consumption of wrapper

jokes.text: I don't want your car to explode and I don't want you to go into a coma and I don't want to stab you in the face with a giant fork. What? Oh, that's not you. I've been threatening a lot of people lately.
jokes.setup: Liz's heartfelt apology
jokes.punchline: Revealing the fork threat was for someone else, casual admission of widespread threatening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - mixing genuine emotion with casual admission of violent tendencies

jokes.text: And I wolfed my Teamster sub for you. Wait, no. Is that a saying?
jokes.setup: Liz trying to express her sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Realizing she may have invented a sexual euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good Liz self-awareness moment - accidentally creating innuendo then questioning herself
